focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
open_hooks_to_carry: Three almost-confessions: to Dani (clearing Bao's desk), to Priya (her near-resignation), to Marcus (direct question about pipeline anomalies)
Canopy announces hiring 500 additional labelers to rebuild corrupted data — Nick's sabotage strengthened the human layer
The final scene: Nick alone, paint and canvas, staring at the blank surface, reaching for the laptop instead, logging in, opening a new batch, starting to rate

payoffs_to_land.description: Nick's withdrawal from intimacy in Ch 13 repeats as withdrawal from confession — the same incapacity for honesty, now with higher stakes
The industry's pattern of scaling the human layer under pressure (seeded Ch 7) produces the ironic final outcome: sabotage causes MORE hiring, strengthening the system

prev_tail_excerpt:
...ield. He was scoring a hallway while a woman broke down six feet away. The rubric didn't have a category for that, either.

He couldn't go in. He couldn't undo it. The confession was right there — his lips parted, his lungs filled — and then the air left without words, as it had been doing for months, because the truth had become a shape his mouth couldn't form.

On the other side of the wall, the breathing began to slow. Not because it was better, but because that was what the body did — found a floor, a survivable rate, a rhythm that would keep the blood moving even when the mind couldn't hold itself together. After a minute the faucet shut off. After another minute he heard paper towels being pulled from the dispenser one at a time, the small mechanical click of the holder with each sheet.

He walked away.

He went back to his desk. The evaluation portal was still open and the batch was still waiting, the five-point scale laid out in its usual row. A fresh image sat in the queue as if Bao had not just driven away, as if Dani were not at her desk measuring every click against a court date, as if Tommy's letter and Priya's breathing belonged to some other floor.

The next image in the queue was a generated sunset over water — two light sources that contradicted each other, a horizon that bent where physics wouldn't allow it, reflections that bore no relation to the objects above them. His hand stayed on the mouse long enough for the cursor to drift to the lower corner of the screen. He dragged it back, wiped his thumb on his jeans, and stared at the water until the contradictions separated themselves: a second light source where none belonged, a horizon bent against physics, reflections broken loose from the objects above them.

He marked it down. Then he clicked Next.
secondary_character_beats: Dani's grief for Bao — cleaning out his desk station, finding his handwritten evaluation notes with too-detailed color observations
Priya telling Nick she's thinking of quitting, her eyes red — the human cost of management pressure, now compounded by the investigation
Marcus asking Nick directly whether he has 'noticed anything anomalous' — the question as an offered lifeline Nick refuses to take
state_deltas_required: Nick faces three natural openings to confess and chooses silence each time; the industry responds to his sabotage by hiring more labelers

Nick's silence becomes self-destruction — his relationships, his integrity, and his identity as an artist are all casualties; the system absorbs sabotage the way it absorbs art, by rating it and moving on
